name: Valley Premier Bank
description_short: Community bank operating in Minnesota since 1892, offering traditional banking products with employee ownership and local decision-making.
description_long:
Valley Premier Bank is the rebranded name of State Bank of Hawley, a community-focused financial institution established in 1892 in Hawley, Minnesota. The bank expanded to Moorhead in 1994 and adopted its current name in 2014 to reflect its service area across multiple communities. The bank is notable for its unique ownership structure: employees acquired majority ownership through an Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) in 2009, giving staff direct financial incentive in customer satisfaction and bank performance.

The bank offers comprehensive traditional banking services including commercial loans, real estate loans, consumer loans, checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, safe deposit boxes, and IRAs. They provide modern digital conveniences through online banking, mobile banking, and bill pay services. Both the Hawley and Moorhead locations offer identical service menus, positioning themselves as full-service community banks rather than specialized lenders.

Valley Premier Bank distinguishes itself through genuine local ownership and 130+ years of community presence. Unlike regional or national chains, all decisions are made by local owners and staff who live in the communities they serve. The ESOP structure creates alignment between employee interests and customer service quality—workers benefit directly from customer retention and satisfaction. The company emphasizes long-term relationship-building over transactional banking.

This is a straightforward community bank appropriate for customers seeking traditional banking services with local accountability. The main limitation is geographic scope—they operate only two physical locations in Minnesota. They do not specialize in credit repair, debt relief, or alternative lending products. For customers requiring services beyond standard deposit accounts and conventional lending, this bank may not be the optimal choice. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Employee majority ownership via ESOP since 2009 aligns staff incentives with customer service quality
130+ years of continuous operation demonstrates stability and deep community integration
Locally-owned and operated with decision-making authority held by community residents
Comprehensive traditional banking suite including commercial, real estate, and consumer loans
Modern digital banking capabilities: online banking, mobile banking, and bill pay services
Two full-service branch locations offering identical products and services
Safe deposit boxes and IRA products available alongside standard deposit accounts
cons: Limited geographic reach with only two physical locations in Minnesota (Hawley and Moorhead)
No specialized lending products for credit-challenged borrowers, debt consolidation, or alternative credit building
Website provides minimal information about loan terms, APRs, fees, or product-specific details
No apparent online account opening or digital-first onboarding process mentioned
No mention of business banking features, merchant services, or commercial-specific tools
best_for: Individuals and families in the Hawley and Moorhead, Minnesota area seeking traditional banking with local ownership
Small to mid-size businesses in the service area needing commercial loans and business banking relationships
Customers who prioritize relationship banking and local decision-making over national bank convenience
Depositors seeking safe deposit boxes, CDs, and IRAs through a stable community institution
diagnosis: Valley Premier Bank is best for existing customers in the Hawley and Moorhead area or those relocating to these communities who want traditional banking services with genuine local ownership. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—if you're not in their service area or need specialized credit products (debt relief, credit building, consolidation), this institution cannot help; you would need to explore regional or national alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
